description: In an apartment kitchen with cardboard boxes, Ross stands holding a blue can while Chandler stands nearby holding a small baseball bat. An off-screen speaker says, "That is why we don't do it."
Chandler turns his head slightly toward Ross.
Chandler, with the bat resting on his hip, says, "I don't think that was my point."
Ross gestures with his free hand and looks at Chandler.
Ross points his finger and says, "You know what the scariest part is? What if there's only one woman for everybody? You know?"
Ross opens his arms wide and continues, "I mean, what if you get one woman and that's it?"
Ross lowers his arms.
Ross points his finger down, raises his eyebrows, and says, "Unfortunately, in my case, there was only one woman for her."
Chandler looks toward the right as Joey walks into the kitchen holding two blue cans and a pizza box.
Joey stops, holding the cans up, and asks, "What are you talking about?"
Ross replies, "One woman," looking at Joey.
Joey looks at Ross.
Joey walks past Chandler toward Ross. "That's like saying there's only one flavor of ice cream for you," Joey says, handing a can to Chandler.
Joey opens his can and says, "Let me tell you something, Ross."
Joey wraps his arm around Ross's shoulders, gesturing with his can, and says, "There's lots of flavors out there."
Joey continues, "There's rocky road and cookie dough and bing cherry vanilla," looking closely at Ross.
Joey shakes Ross slightly. "You can get them with jimmy's or nuts," Joey continues.
Joey points his can at Ross and says, "A whipped cream."
Joey taps Ross on the chest with his free hand. "This is the best thing that ever happened to you," Joey says.
Joey removes his arm from Ross and steps back, saying, "You got married, you were like, what, eight?"
Chandler looks at Ross. Ross looks down, holding his can.
Joey claps Ross on the shoulder. "Welcome back to the world. Grab a spoon," Joey says.
Ross lowers his head.
Ross says, "I honestly don't know if I'm hungry or horny." Chandler points at Ross and adds, "Stay out of my freezer."
The setting changes to an Asian-style restaurant. Monica and Paul sit across from each other at a table, eating with chopsticks. A waitress in a red kimono walks past.
Paul looks at his food and says, "Ever since she walked out on me," then pauses.
Monica stops chewing and looks at Paul.
Monica leans forward slightly and asks, "What?"
Paul looks up at her.
Monica points her chopsticks at Paul's plate and asks, "You want to spell it out with noodles?"
Paul smiles, looking up, and replies, "No, it's more of a fifth date kind of revelation."
Monica smiles widely at Paul.
Monica asks, "So there's going to be a fifth date?"
Paul maintains his smile.
Paul asks, "Isn't there?" Monica replies, "Yeah, I think there is," maintaining her smile.
Monica gestures with her hand.
Monica asks, "What were you going to say?"
Paul lifts a small cup to his mouth while Monica watches him.
Paul lowers the cup and says, "Well,"
Paul sets his chopsticks down and rubs his hands together.
Paul looks at Monica and says, "ever since she left me,"
Monica raises her cup to her mouth.
Monica takes a sip as Paul continues, "I haven't been able to perform"
Monica holds the cup near her mouth, looking at Paul.
Paul adds, "sexually," then rubs his forehead with his hand. Monica abruptly spits her drink back into her cup.
Monica grabs a cloth napkin and wipes her mouth. Paul raises his hands and offers her another napkin.
Monica wipes her mouth and says, "I'm so sorry. Okay, I know being spit on is probably not what you need right now."
Monica places her hand behind her neck, looking at Paul. Paul looks down.
Monica asks, "How long?"
Paul replies, "Two years," looking up at her.
Monica stares at Paul.
Monica widens her eyes and says, "Wow."
Monica looks down slightly.
Monica leans forward slightly and says, "I'm glad you smashed your watch."
Paul smiles and looks down, then back up at Monica.
Paul asks, "Well, you still think you might want that fifth date?"
Monica smiles, maintaining eye contact with Paul.
Monica nods slightly and says, "Yeah, I do."
The scene changes to a living room. Rachel sits on a sofa, facing a television showing a wedding scene.
Rachel hugs a white wedding dress against her chest. A voice from the television says, "We are gathered here to join together..."
The voice continues, "...joni Louise Cunningham and Charles." Rachel keeps her gaze fixed on the television.
Rachel watches the television, holding the dress.
Voices from the television overlap, saying, "Chachi. Chachi."
The television voice continues, "Chachi. Our caller."
The television voice adds, "In the bonds of holy matrimony, merit is the most sacred." Rachel watches the television without moving.
Rachel breathes in deeply.
Rachel exhales and says, "Joni love chachi," her voice trembling slightly.
Rachel shifts the dress in her arms and says, "That's the difference," tears visible in her eyes.
Rachel rests her hand on her cheek. The scene changes to Ross sitting on a window ledge behind vertical blinds, holding a blue can.
Ross looks forward and says, "Grab a spoon."
Ross holds the blinds open with one hand and asks, "Do you know how long it's been since I grabbed a spoon?"
Ross tilts his head slightly and asks, "Did the words Billy, don't be a hero mean anything to you?"
Chandler stands leaning against the kitchen counter while Joey sits on a box on the floor. Both men look toward Ross in silence.
In an apartment area containing unpacked cardboard boxes, Joey stands up and grabs a black jacket while Chandler sits on the floor leaning against a box, holding a blue can.
Joey slides his arms into the jacket and says, "Great story, but I gotta go. I got a date with Andrea. Angela."
Chandler points slightly with his free hand and corrects him, "Andrea."
Leaning against the box, Chandler continues, "Oh, man. Andrea's the screamer. Angela has cats."
Joey points back at Chandler and says, "Right. Thanks. It's Julie. I'm out of".
Joey turns and walks out the door. Ross sits on a window ledge, holding the vertical blinds open with both hands.
Ross looks forward and says, "know. Here's the thing. Even if I could get it together enough to, you know, to ask a woman out,".
Ross continues holding the blinds open and shifts his gaze.
Ross turns his head to look out the window and asks, "who am I going to ask?"
Ross remains looking out the window. The setting changes to Rachel sitting at night by a brick-walled window, her chin resting on her hand. Instrumental music begins playing.
Rachel looks out the window as a male vocal sings, "If you ever need holding,".
Rachel remains still, gazing out the window.
The male vocal sings, "call my name".
Rachel blinks slowly while looking out the window.
The vocal sings, "I'll be there."
Rachel continues resting her chin on her hand, gazing outside.
The vocal sings, "Have you ever need holding?" The setting changes to a daytime view of the New York City skyline seen from underneath a bridge.
The setting changes to a daytime view of a city street corner with a coffee shop on the ground floor of a brick building.
Inside an apartment, Chandler and Joey sit at a round wooden table. Rachel walks over to them holding a glass coffee pot.
Rachel stands by the table and says, "It's amazing. I mean, I have never made coffee before in my entire life." Seated at the table, Chandler says, "That is amazing. Congratulations."
Rachel pours coffee into Chandler's mug and says, "You know, I figure if I can make coffee, there isn't anything I can't do."
Chandler looks up at Rachel and says, "No, I think it's if I can invade Poland, there isn't anything I can't".
Joey gestures with his hand toward the kitchen.
Joey points and says, "listen. Why? You're on a roll if you feel like you gotta make, like, a Western omelet or something."
Joey lifts his pink mug and takes a sip of the coffee. His brow furrows and his lips press together tightly as he lowers the mug.
Joey pushes his mug toward the center of the table, then pushes Chandler's mug forward as he says, "Although, actually, I'm really not that hungry."
Monica opens a purple bedroom door and walks into the main room, wearing a dress with a sweater tied around her waist.
Monica stops, looks toward Chandler and Joey, and says, "Oh, good. Lenny and Squiggy are here."
Rachel walks past the table, carrying the coffee pot.
Paul walks out of the bedroom behind Monica, adjusting his belt, and says, "Morning."
Joey and Chandler reply, "Good morning."
Paul finishes adjusting his belt and grabs a dark jacket from a chair. Chandler and Joey watch him.
Rachel says, "Morning, Paul." Chandler says, "Hello, Paul."
Joey points a finger at him and says, "Hi. Paul, is it?"
Paul and Monica walk toward the apartment's green front door. Paul pulls the door open.
Paul steps into the hallway and turns to face Monica, who stands in the doorway. Rachel watches them from the kitchen area.
Paul looks at Monica and says, "Thank you."
Monica smiles at him.
Paul adds, "Thank you so much."
Monica says, "No, I'm telling you, last night was like all my birthdays, both graduations, plus the barn raising scene in Witness."
Monica smiles broadly. Chandler, Rachel, and Joey sit silently, their eyes fixed on Paul and Monica.
Joey turns his body in the chair to get a better view of the doorway.
Paul says, "We'll talk later."
Monica replies, "Yeah."
Paul leans in and kisses Monica. Chandler, Rachel, and Joey lean forward slightly, continuing to watch them.
Paul pulls back from the kiss and says, "Thank you."
Paul turns and walks down the hallway. Monica closes the door and leans back against it with a smile.
As Monica turns toward the room, Chandler, Rachel, and Joey quickly avert their gaze and look away. Monica walks toward the kitchen.
Joey turns to Monica and says, "That wasn't a real date."
Monica stops walking and looks at Joey.
Monica replies, "What the hell do you do on a real date."
Monica points her hand toward Joey, her jaw set.
Monica says, "Shut up and put my table back."
Monica turns and walks into the kitchen. Chandler and Joey grab the edges of the table and slide it slightly across the floor.
Chandler stands up, buttoning his vest, and says, "All right, kids, I got to get to work. If I don't input those numbers,".
Chandler walks over to the kitchen counter.
Chandler turns back toward the room and says, "it doesn't make much of a difference."
Rachel holds a mug in both hands and watches Chandler gather his things.
Rachel asks, "So, like, you guys all have jobs?"
Monica turns toward Rachel.
Monica says, "Yeah, we all have jobs. See, that's how we buy stuff."
Joey sits at the table and points to his own chest.
Joey says, "Yeah, I'm an actor."
Rachel smiles and looks toward Joey.
Rachel says, "Wow. Have I seen you in anything?" Joey replies, "Oh, I doubt it. Mostly regional work."
Monica steps forward and says, "Okay, unless you happen to catch the Wee Ones production of Pinocchio at the little Theater in the park."
Joey stares fixedly at Monica with his lips pressed together tightly.
Joey points a finger and says, "It was a job." Chandler steps forward and says, "All right, look, Geppetto, I'm a real live boy."
Joey stands up abruptly and walks away from the table. Chandler smiles and heads toward the front door.
Walking toward the door, Joey says, "I will not take this abuse." Chandler turns to him and says, "You're right. I'm sorry."
Chandler stands by the door, looking at Joey.
Chandler sings, "Once I was a wooden boy a little."
Chandler turns and walks out the front door. Joey remains standing in the apartment.
In an apartment living room, Joey walks toward the open front door. Chandler stands near the door, and Rachel stands nearby in a bathrobe. Monica sits at a round table.
Joey exits the apartment and says, "We should both know that he's a dead man."
Chandler turns to follow Joey out the door as audience laughter is heard.
Rachel calls out, "Oh, Chandler," as he pauses in the doorway.
Chandler turns around and gestures with his hands before exiting. Rachel walks over to the table where Monica is sitting.
Monica looks up at Rachel and asks, "So how are you doing today? You sleep okay? You talk to Barry?"
Rachel sits down at the table.
Rachel replies, "I can't stop smiling."
Monica rests her chin on her hand.
Monica says, "I can see that. You look like you slept with a hanger in your mouth."
Monica laughs, placing her hands on her chest, accompanied by audience laughter.
Rachel smiles and leans forward, resting her arms on the table.
Rachel says, "I know he is. Just so" before Monica asks, "you remember you and Tony DeMarco?"
Rachel gestures with her hands and says, "Oh, yeah. Well, it's like that. With feelings."
Monica looks silently at Rachel.
Monica says, "Oh, wow," over audience laughter.
Monica asks, "Are you in trouble." Rachel says, "Big time." Monica asks, "Want a wedding dress? Hardly." Rachel says, "You this time."
Monica says, "I think we're getting a little ahead of ourselves here," while gesturing with her hands, as audience laughter is heard.
Monica stands up from the table, brushing her hands together, and says, "Okay, I am just going to get up, go to work and not think about him all day."
Monica turns to walk away.
Monica stops, turns back toward Rachel, and says, "Or else I'm just going to get up and go to work."
Rachel stands up from the table.
Rachel says, "Oh, wish me luck." Monica asks, "What for?" Rachel gestures toward the door and says, "I'm going to go get one of those job things."
Rachel gestures with her hand and walks toward the kitchen area as Monica exits through the front door.
The scene changes to a city skyline illuminated at night.
The scene changes to the exterior of a storefront; a glowing sign reads "Iridium RESTAURANT" as a taxi drives past.
The scene changes to a restaurant kitchen where Monica and another chef, both in white uniforms, are cooking. The other chef says, "Hey, Monica." Monica replies, "Frannie."
Monica stirs a pot and asks, "Welcome back. How was Florida? You had sex, didn't you?"
The other chef stops stirring and looks at Monica.
The other chef asks, "How do you do that?"
Monica gestures with a wooden spoon and says, "Oh, I hate you. I'm pushing my Aunt Roz through Parrot Jungle and you're having sex." Audience laughter follows.
The other chef resumes stirring.
Monica looks at the other chef and asks, "So who?"
The other chef turns her head toward Monica.
The other chef asks, "You know Paul?"
Monica replies, "Paul? The wine guy?"
The other chef says, "Oh, yeah," and Monica says, "I know Paul."
Monica smiles and looks steadily at the other chef.
The other chef asks, "You mean, you know Paul like I know Paul?"
Monica stops smiling, points to herself, and says, "Are you kidding? I take credit for Paul. You know, before me there was no snapping his turtle for"
Monica finishes her sentence, saying, "two years."
The scene changes to a coffee shop where a group of friends is gathered. Joey sits on the arm of a couch, throwing his arms wide open and laughing over audience laughter.
Joey says, "Of course, it was a"
Monica throws her hands down in front of her as audience laughter continues.
Monica, standing behind the couch, gestures with her hands and asks, "why. Why would anybody do something like that?"
Ross, seated in an armchair with a book in his lap, gestures with his hand and says, "I assume we're looking for an answer more sophisticated than to get you into bed."
Monica turns and throws her hands down over audience laughter.
Monica says, "I hate men. I hate men." Phoebe, sitting on the floor by the coffee table, replies, "Oh, no, don't hate."
Phoebe says, "You don't want to put that out in the universe."
Monica gestures toward herself with both hands.
Monica asks, "Is it me?"
Joey turns his head toward Monica.
Monica raises her hands and asks, "Is it like I have some sort of beacon that only dogs and men with severe emotional problems can hear?"
Phoebe gestures with a marker and says, "All right,"
Phoebe continues, "come here. Give me your feet."
Monica steps around the couch and sits down next to where Phoebe is sitting on the floor.
Phoebe lifts Monica's feet into her lap, wrapping them with a white napkin. Monica looks down at her feet.
Monica says, "You know,"
Ross looks toward Monica as Joey begins to laugh.
Joey leans back, laughing, and says, "I can't believe you didn't know it was a line," accompanied by audience laughter.
Rachel walks into the coffee shop carrying several large shopping bags.
Rachel says, "Guess what?"
Ross turns his head toward Rachel.
Ross asks, "You got a job." Rachel replies, "Are you kidding? I'm trained for nothing."
Rachel walks over to stand directly behind the couch.
Rachel says, "I was laughed out of twelve interviews today."
Chandler, sitting in an armchair, says, "And yet you're surprisingly upbeat."
Rachel pulls a large box from a shopping bag and says, "Well, you would be too if you found"
Rachel places the box on the back of the couch and says, "Joan and David boots on sale 50% off," as audience laughter is heard.
Chandler looks up at Rachel.
Chandler says, "Oh, how well you know me."
Rachel opens the box and pulls out a dark leather boot.
Rachel holds the boot up and says, "They're my new. I don't need a job. I don't need my parents. I've got great boots." Monica asks, "Boots?" Audience laughter plays.
Monica smiles and touches the boot in Rachel's hands.
Monica asks, "How'd you pay for them?" Rachel answers, "Credit card." Monica asks, "And who pays for,"
Rachel looks at Monica with a slight smile.
Rachel says, "um, my?"
Rachel's smile fades as she looks down, with audience laughter in the background.
In an apartment kitchen and living area, Rachel, Monica, Joey, Chandler, and Ross sit around a round wooden table. Rachel leans over four credit cards, holding scissors near a plant on the table.

Rachel sits up and gestures with both hands. "Oh, come on, you guys. Is this really necessary? I mean, I can stop charging anytime I want." Monica leans forward and replies, "Come on, Rachel. You can't"
Monica continues, "live off your parents your whole life."
Rachel gestures with her hands again and says, "I know that. That's why I was getting married." Audience laughter is audible.
Phoebe, wearing pigtails, sits on an adjacent armchair and leans forward toward the table.
Phoebe speaks up, "Come on, give her a break. It's hard being on your own for the first time." Rachel nods slightly and replies, "Thank you."
Phoebe gestures with her hands as she says, "You're welcome. I remember when I first came to this city. I was 14."
Rachel watches her. Phoebe continues, "My mom had just killed herself. And my stepdad was back"
Phoebe adds, "in prison. And I got here, and I didn't know anybody."
Phoebe continues gesturing with her left hand while the others listen silently.
Phoebe states, "And I ended up living with this albino guy who was, like, cleaning windshields outside Port Authority."
Phoebe continues, "And then he killed himself. And then" as Rachel stares at her without moving.
Phoebe lowers her hands and says, "I found aromatherapy. So believe me, I know exactly how you feel." Rachel stares with parted lips as audience laughter plays.
Ross, standing near the kitchen counter behind Rachel, leans forward slightly and looks at her.
Ross says, "The word you're looking for is"
Ross pauses, maintaining his gaze on Rachel.
Ross finishes, "anyway." Audience laughter follows.
Rachel turns back toward the table. Monica picks up a pair of scissors with orange handles and extends them toward Rachel.
Monica asks, "All right, you ready?" Rachel pulls back slightly, raising her hands. "No, I'm not ready. How can I be ready?"
Joey, sitting on a counter behind Monica, leans on his knees and says, "Hey, Rach, you ready to jump out of the airplane without a parachute?"
Rachel shakes her head, "Come on, I can't do this." Monica says, "You can. I know you can." Rachel replies, "I don't think so."
Chandler sits next to Monica. Ross leans forward and says, "Come on, you made coffee. You can do anything."
Rachel looks down at the scissors in her hands, holding them poised open over a credit card.
Chandler begins chanting, "Cut."
The group repeatedly chants "Cut" together. Rachel closes the scissors, snapping the card in half.
The group cheers and claps as Rachel drops the pieces of the card onto the table.
Rachel holds up another card and says, "You know what?"
Rachel sets the remaining cards and the scissors down onto the table.
Rachel says, "I think we can just leave it at that. Kind of like a symbolic gesture."
Monica gestures toward the table and says, "Rachel, that was a library card." Audience laughter follows.
Rachel looks down, her eyes widening slightly. Ross picks up a credit card and holds it toward her as Chandler begins chanting "Cut" again.
The group resumes chanting. Rachel picks up the scissors and cuts the remaining credit cards in half one by one.
Chandler points a finger up and says, "You know, if you listen very closely, you can hear a thousand retailers scream." Audience laughter plays.
The group cheers and claps again as Rachel finishes cutting the last card. Monica stands up and walks behind Rachel, wrapping her arms around her shoulders.
Monica hugs Rachel and says, "Welcome to the real world. It sucks. You're going to love it."
The scene changes to an exterior nighttime view of city buildings.
The setting changes back to the living room. The television displays a choir singing with an American flag overlay. Monica, Ross, and Rachel sit on the couches watching the TV.
Monica aims a remote at the television, turning it off. "That's it."
Ross rubs his eyes as Monica looks at him.
Monica asks, "You want to crash on the couch?" Ross stands up and replies, "No, I got to go home sometime." Monica asks, "You're going to be okay?" Ross nods, "Yeah."
Rachel quickly leans over the coffee table and picks something up from the floor.
Rachel holds up a wristwatch and says, "Hang on. What's? What I just found on the floor."
Monica turns her head to look at the watch.
Monica asks, "What?"
Monica stares at the watch in Rachel's hand.
Monica points at the watch and says, "That's Paul's watch. You just put it back where you found it."
Monica stands up from the couch and walks toward the hallway.
Monica says, "Oh, boy. All right. Good night, everybody." Ross says, "All right," watching her walk away.
Monica shuts her bedroom door. Ross walks over to the adjacent couch and sits down on the edge, facing Rachel.
Ross and Rachel look at each other. Rachel smiles and sighs softly.
Ross looks down at an Oreo cookie in his hands. He twists and splits the cookie in half.
Ross offers one half of the cookie toward Rachel. She shakes her head and says, "No."
Ross pulls his hand back slightly, saying, "Sorry. Oh, no." Rachel gestures with both hands and replies, "Go. Really, I don't want it split it."
Ross nods and says, "Okay."
Ross presses the cookie halves back together and looks down at them. Rachel smiles gently at him.
Ross looks up at Rachel and says, "Thanks. You probably didn't know this,"
Ross continues, "but back in high school, I had a major crush on you."
Rachel smiles softly, lowering her gaze slightly.
Looking back up at Ross, Rachel says, "I knew."
Ross's eyes widen slightly.
Ross raises his eyebrows and asks, "You did?"
Ross tilts his head and smiles slightly while looking at Rachel.
Ross gestures with the cookie and says, "I always figured you just thought I was Monica's geeky older brother." Rachel smiles and replies, "I did."
Ross looks down at his hands, saying, "Oh."
Ross gives a small, close-lipped smile and stares ahead as audience laughter plays.
In a living room, Ross and Rachel sit side by side on a sofa, each holding a cookie.
Ross looks down at his cookie, then up at Rachel, and says, "Listen, do you think and try not to let my intense vulnerability become any kind of a factor here,"
Rachel smiles slightly.
Ross continues, "but do you think it would be okay if I asked you out sometime, maybe?"
Rachel keeps her gaze on Ross.
Rachel smiles and says, "Yeah?"
Ross nods.
Rachel says, "Maybe."
Ross looks down at his hands, then back up at her.
Ross says, "Okay."
Ross twists his cookie apart.
Ross points at Rachel with a piece of the cookie and says, "It okay. Maybe I will."
Rachel laughs. Audience laughter is audible.
Rachel stands up, saying, "Good night." Ross replies, "Good night."
Rachel walks past the television toward the hallway.
Ross says, "Good night" while watching her leave.
Ross sits alone on the sofa, smiling, and eats a piece of the cookie.
Ross stands up and walks around the coffee table.
Ross picks up his jacket from a chair and drapes it over his arm.
Ross walks toward the front door. Monica enters the living room from her bedroom, wearing blue pajamas.
Monica looks at Ross and asks, "Hey. What's with you?"
Ross opens the front door and turns back toward Monica.
Ross smiles and says, "I just grabbed a spoon."
Ross exits the apartment, pulling the door closed behind him. Monica smiles.
Monica remains standing in the kitchen area. The screen then turns black.
The setting changes to a coffee shop, where Ross, Monica, Joey, Chandler, and Phoebe sit together around a large orange sofa.
Monica and Phoebe speak simultaneously, saying, "I can't believe what I'm hearing here. I believe what I'm hearing here."
Monica gestures with a card in her hand, looking at Phoebe, and says, "What? I said you had an I said you have would you stop?"
Phoebe asks, "Oh, was I doing it again?"
Monica replies, "Yes,"
Monica turns her gaze toward Joey, who is sitting on a reversed chair.
Monica says to Joey, "I said that you had a nice butt. It's just not a great butt."
Joey replies, "You wouldn't know a great butt if it came up and bit you."
Ross points a finger and says, "There's an image."
Rachel walks up to the group, wearing a denim shirt, an apron, and holding a coffee pot.
Rachel asks, "Would anybody like more coffee?"
Chandler looks at Rachel.
Chandler asks, "Did you make it or are you just serving it?" Rachel replies, "I'm just serving it." Chandler says, "Yeah."
Rachel pours coffee into Chandler's extended mug.
Chandler says, "Kids, new dream. I'm in Las Vegas." An off-screen man calls out, "Miss, more coffee?"
Rachel turns toward a man in a brown jacket at a nearby table.
The man hands Rachel money and says, "Excuse me. Could you give to that guy over there?"
Rachel takes the money.
An off-screen voice says, "Go ahead."
Rachel turns back to the group.
Rachel says, "Thank you."
Rachel says, "Sorry."
Chandler gestures with his hands and says, "Okay. Las Vegas. Okay, so I'm in Las Vegas."
The group looks at Chandler.
Chandler says, "I'm Liza Minnelli."
Audience laughter is audible as the group reacts.
The screen turns black, then a title card for "Bright Kauffman Crane productions" appears.
The "WARNER BROS. TELEVISION" shield logo appears over a cloudy sky background.
The logo remains on the screen until the video concludes.
